name: opencode-memory-enhanced
description: Advanced autonomous memory plugin: auto-remember, predictive recall, knowledge graph, hybrid FTS5+vector search, session scan, curated memory, procedural skills, all-local
author: MrCarb0n

tools.name: memory-query
tools.description: Search, browse, and explore memories and entities. Modes: recall (default) — FTS5+vector search with pagination; inject — context injection for a task; browse — paginated list with detail/delete; entity — knowledge graph entity search with relationship traversal

tools.name: memory-store
tools.description: Store memories, import data, and manage procedural skills. Modes: store (default) — save with auto-tags, entities, dedup; import — bulk import from JSON export; skill — manage skills (add/apply/list)

tools.name: memory-learn
tools.description: Store structured facts and track conversation intent. Modes: learn (default) — fact with entity tracking, relationships, pattern detection; arc — track conversation intent and topics

tools.name: memory-info
tools.description: System information and configuration. Modes: status (default) — stats, health, embedding model; config — view/update settings with schema validation; timeline — activity history

tools.name: memory-curated
tools.description: Manage curated memory stores and approval queue. Modes: list (default) — show entries and pending writes; add — add entry to agent_note/user_profile; replace — replace matching entry; remove — remove matching entry; approve — approve pending write; reject — reject pending write

tools.name: memory-maintain
tools.description: System maintenance tasks. Modes: optimize (default) — prune stale, dedup, backfill embeddings, vacuum; dedup — find and merge duplicates with threshold/dryRun; conflicts — detect contradictory feedback memories

tools.name: memory-export
tools.description: Export memories and knowledge graph. Modes: json (default) — full export with entities/relationships/patterns; md — Markdown; svg — inline SVG graph; dot — Graphviz DOT graph

tools.name: memory-scan
tools.description: Scan past session logs from OpenCode DB or API. Args: limit (number, default 50), full (boolean, scan all sessions), source (string: 'db', 'api', or 'auto')
